title: Left, right, straight — near and far
pedagogy: Vocabulary: ซ้าย (LOW ซ + mai tho → high), ขวา (true ขว cluster: high ข + live → rising), ตรง (true ตร cluster: mid ต + live ง → mid), ใกล้ (mid ก + mai tho → falling), ไกล (mid ก + live ไ◌ → mid). klai_set drills the near/far minimal pair — same sound, one tone apart, opposite meanings.

exercises.title: Taxi Thai is three words — and one trap
body: ซ้าย left, ขวา right, ตรงไป straight ahead. That's enough to co-pilot any ride in the kingdom.
The trap: ใกล้ (falling) means near, ไกล (mid) means far. Same sound to a beginner's ear, opposite bills at the meter.
Written, they're easy — ใ for near, ไ for far. Spoken, only the tone tells them apart. Time to train your ear.

exercises.explanation: ตรงไป — straight-go. Add ครับ/ค่ะ when it's a driver, not a friend.
